result:
Done — the spec is fully implemented and verified. I added the three missing operations to `CsvRow.calr` in the Calor DSL, leaving `NeedsQuoting` untouched:

- **`EscapeField`** binds the result of `NeedsQuoting`, and when quoting is needed it doubles internal quotes with `(replace field "\"" "\"\"")` and wraps the field in quotes; otherwise it returns the field verbatim.
- **`JoinRow`** iterates the fields with `§EACH`, escaping each and joining with commas via a first-element flag, so the empty list yields the empty string. It takes `List<str>`, which the compiler maps to `List<string>` (the `[str]` array form I tried first broke the pinned `List<string>` surface, so I switched).
- **`SplitRow`** is a single-pass scanner with an in-quotes flag: inside quotes, a doubled `""` contributes one literal quote and an un-doubled quote closes the field; outside quotes, commas split fields and a leading quote enters quoted mode. The final field is always pushed, so an empty line gives one empty field and a trailing comma gives a trailing empty field. It declares `§E{mut}` for the list pushes.

The build is clean (0 warnings, 0 errors) and the generated C# signatures match the pinned public surface exactly (`JoinRow(List<string>)`, `SplitRow → List<string>`). I also ran a runtime round-trip check from a scratch console project (deleted afterward, not part of the repo): 11 round-trip cases covering commas, quotes, doubled quotes, CR/LF, empty and trailing-empty fields, plus the `EscapeField` edge cases and the empty-line/empty-list special cases — all passed. Two of the ten build cycles were used.
